concerns.text: Task T019 ('Implement code/models/kairos_adapter.py to load pre-trained weights') is a consumer of an external artifact (Kairos weights) that has no corresponding producer task in the list. The plan/spec assumes these weights exist, but without a task to download/verify them, T019 cannot execute. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule for external dependencies.

Tasks T027 and T028 (Stability Analysis) consume model outputs from US2 (T020/T021) AND a 'continuous visual-modality baseline' run. The tasks list lacks a specific task to generate or load this baseline (required by FR-008 and Assumption about baseline comparison). Without a producer task for the baseline data, the consumer tasks in Phase 5 cannot calculate 'relative degradation' as specified.

Task T011 (Download LIBERO) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, it is the sole producer of the raw HDF5 data required by T012 (Quantize) and T013 (Noise). While T011 is listed before T012/13, the [P] tag implies it can run in parallel with other tasks. If T012/13 are also marked [P] (as they are), a parallel execution engine might attempt to run T012 before T011 completes, causing a race condition. The [P] tag on T012/T013 is unsafe without explicit dependency enforcement or a 'wait-for-download' mechanism.

Task T027 description omits the specific horizons (100, 500, 1000) mandated by Constitution Principle VII and the Plan. While the task exists, the description lacks the specific artifact-flow parameters required to ensure the output matches the downstream statistical analysis needs.
